source.title: BSEE Accident Investigation Report - Structural Damage to Crane/Top Drive

context.region: Gulf of Mexico, Alaminos Canyon Block 815

event.incident_type: Equipment failure due to human error
actions_taken: Sea floor search for lost drill string section
Drill string weight indicator data log reviewed
Iron Roughneck maintenance and calibration logs reviewed
DS-1 Cat 5 inspection conducted on HWDP
event.summary: On 1 April 2022, during well abandonment operations on the Helix Q-4000 semi-submersible rig in well SA001 (G-19409 Lease, Alaminos Canyon 815), four sections of 5-7/8 inch Heavy Weight Drill Pipe (HWDP) and the Bottom Hole Assembly (BHA) weighing 6000 pounds and 123 feet long disconnected from the drill string and dropped to the sea floor. The disconnection occurred on 31 March 2022 at 15:42 when a loss of 5000 pounds was recorded on the drill string weight indicator, but was not identified until 1 April 2022 at 08:20 via ROV monitoring. The root cause was determined to be an improperly torqued drill pipe connection. The lost equipment came to rest 92 meters from well center and 377 meters from the center of the safe operating zone. No subsea infrastructure damage was sustained. Recovery and inspection costs totaled $2,300,000.
recommendations: Lessee needs to ensure all drill string connections are properly torqued to required specifications

hazards.name: Pressurized drill string with heavy components
Subsea infrastructure in proximity to well operations

threats.name: Improper torque application on drill pipe connections
Rig movement and sea currents causing unscrew of drill string
Failure to detect loss of drill string weight

consequences.name: Loss of drill string section to sea floor

controls.name: Iron Roughneck torque application and verification
controls.barrier_role: Ensure drill pipe connections are properly torqued to specification
controls.lod_basis: Primary control to prevent improper torque

supporting_text: Operator failed to properly torque the drill pipe connection
Drill crew failed to torque and verify the torque of the connection
All connections were checked and found to be properly torqued using the Iron Roughneck

controls.name: Drill string weight indicator monitoring
controls.barrier_role: Detect loss of drill string weight to identify disconnection
controls.lod_basis: Secondary detection control to identify loss of drill string

supporting_text: Review of the drill string weight indicator data log identified a loss of five thousand pounds on 31 March 2022 at 15:42
The Lessee failed to identify the missing weight from the drill string for over 16 hours
The missing four joints of 5-7/8 inch Heavy Weight Drill Pipe (HWDP) and Bottom Hole Assembly (BHA) was identified as missing on 1 April 2022 at 08:20 am

controls.name: ROV inspection of drill string
controls.barrier_role: Visual inspection to detect disconnection of drill string components
controls.lod_basis: Secondary detection control via remote monitoring

supporting_text: At 08:20 it was identified via the Q-4000 remote operated vehicle (ROV) monitor, the Bottom Hole Assembly (BHA) was no longer attached to the drill string
At 13:30 the MV ROV began a 14-element survey inspection of the subsea infrastructure

controls.name: Subsea infrastructure shut-in procedure
controls.barrier_role: Isolate subsea infrastructure to prevent damage from dropped equipment
controls.lod_basis: Tertiary control to mitigate consequence of dropped equipment

supporting_text: Following notification of the incident to Perdido Spar, the Senior Production Operator shut-in the Silvertip subsea infrastructure
After the MV ROV completed the site survey and found no infrastructure damage

controls.name: Drill pipe connection inspection and maintenance
controls.barrier_role: Ensure drill pipe connections are fit for service
controls.lod_basis: Primary control to prevent defective connections

supporting_text: Maintenance and calibration of the Iron Roughneck was found to be acceptable
Photographic documentation of the threaded end (pin) connection, where the drill string separated, showed no visual evidence of damage or excessive wear
An in-depth DS-1 Cat 5 inspection (measurements and ultrasonic testing) of the threaded box connection was conducted in Houma Louisiana. All measurements were within limits and no defects were found

notes.rules: JSON output only. mentioned fields must be evidence-based. Use null for unknown values.
